title: The Puppies Want Pancakes
logline: Ginger and Joe wake the sisters at dawn begging for pancakes. Sara, Eva, Mama, and Papa end up with a cheerful kitchen morning — counting pancakes, feeding the pups, and laughing together.
learningMoment: Counting 1 to 5 (Sara teaches Eva to count pancakes on the plate)

catchphrases.Sisters_to_Papa: Spin, spin, spin!  (real-family beat — sisters ask Papa to spin them by their hands)
catchphrases.Joe: Yip yip!  (Pomeranian happy yips, NOT 'Spin spin spin')

modeReason: Single-shot per micro-clip = surgical re-renders. A bad 5s shot costs 45 credits to redo, vs 135 if it were one shot inside a 15s multi-shot. ~25% credit savings per episode after typical re-render rate.

arc.label: HOOK
arc.summary: Ginger barks awake the bunk-bed sisters.

arc.label: SETUP
arc.summary: Sisters sit up; Joe runs in to greet Eva.

arc.label: RISING
arc.summary: Sara + Eva + Ginger stampede through the playroom toward the kitchen smell.

arc.label: DISCOVERY
arc.summary: Mama at the stove flipping pancakes; the two dogs sit and beg.

arc.label: FAMILY
arc.summary: Sara and Eva run in to greet Mama.

arc.label: DAD
arc.summary: Papa walks in yawning, tickles Eva, kisses Mama.

arc.label: LEARNING
arc.summary: Sara teaches Eva to count pancakes 1-2-3-4-5.

arc.label: RESOLUTION
arc.summary: Warm payoff at the table — Joe yips, Spin spin spin with Papa.

arc.label: FAMILY_TABLE
arc.summary: Family closer at the dining table — Mama, Papa, Sara eating together.

arc.label: BRUSH_TEETH
arc.summary: Papa brushes teeth with Sara and Eva — healthy teeth!

arc.label: PACK_SCHOOL
arc.summary: Mama packs lunchboxes for Sara and Eva.

arc.label: OFF_TO_SCHOOL
arc.summary: Sara catches school bus #1 (sidewalk). Eva is driven by Mama (front walk).

expectedCreditsTotalNote: Clip 1 (legacy 15s multi-shot) = 135 already spent. New 21 micro-clips × 45 = 945 to submit. Plus ~30% buffer for re-renders ~280 credits. Net ~1,225 credits.

rules.bedroomLayout: ONE dark-wood BUNK BED — Sara on the TOP bunk (pink bedding), Eva on the BOTTOM bunk (lavender bedding). NEVER twin beds, NEVER separate beds.
rules.subjectCapHandling: Each micro-clip can have its OWN ≤3 subjects, rotated per beat. A character not in the shot need not be bound.
rules.nativeAudio: ON for every micro-clip (Kling +50% audio surcharge baked into 45 credit estimate).
rules.masterPrompt: MUST BE EMPTY. Single-shot mode uses the main prompt textarea (which IS the only prompt) — fill it with the micro-clip's `prompt` field.
rules.promptStyle: camera + action with @tags + dialogue. NO appearance, NO voice descriptors, NO pronouns (he/she/it/they), NO group nouns (both/sisters/girls/everyone), NO entry verbs for already-on-screen characters, NO bound-character names spoken inside another character's dialogue.
rules.characterNamesInDialogue: Drop the bound character's name from other characters' dialogue. Sara says 'Come on — pancakes are waiting' NOT 'Come on, Eva — pancakes are waiting' (the latter spawns an extra Eva).
rules.everyoneIsCalledByRealName: Always Sara, Eva, Mama, Papa, Ginger, Joe — never nicknames (no Zhorik, no Evochka, no honey/sweetie).
rules.negativePrompt: extra people, duplicate character, twin, clone, two of the same person, mirrored figure, background extras, crowd, identical sibling
rules.concat: ffmpeg concat the 22 MP4s in arc order with optional 0.2s xfade between adjacent micro-clips. Hard cuts also acceptable for kids' show pacing.
